recipes.title: Cheeseburger
recipes.description: Classic American grilled beef patty with melted cheese on a soft bun, served with lettuce, tomato, and pickles.
steps: Divide the beef into 4 equal balls without overworking, then press into 1.5 cm patties slightly wider than the buns to allow for shrinkage.
Season both sides generously with salt and pepper just before cooking.
Heat a heavy skillet or grill over high heat until smoking, then add a thin film of oil.
Cook the patties for 3 minutes on the first side without moving, flip, and immediately top each with a slice of cheddar.
Cook 2 more minutes for medium, covering the pan briefly to melt the cheese.
Toast the cut sides of the buns in the same pan until golden.
Build each burger with bun, lettuce, tomato slice, patty, onion, pickles, and the top bun, and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Buffalo Wings
recipes.description: Crispy fried chicken wings tossed in a tangy butter and hot sauce, served with celery and blue cheese dressing.
steps: Pat the wings completely dry with paper towels and let them air-dry, uncovered, in the fridge for at least 1 hour for the crispiest skin.
Heat the oil to 175°C in a deep heavy pot.
Fry the wings in batches for 10–12 minutes until deep golden and the internal temperature reaches 75°C.
Drain on a wire rack while you make the sauce.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter, then whisk in the hot sauce, vinegar, Worcestershire, and garlic powder until smooth.
Place the hot wings in a large bowl, pour over the sauce, and toss to coat every piece.
Serve immediately with celery sticks and blue cheese dressing for dipping.

recipes.title: Macaroni and Cheese
recipes.description: Creamy baked pasta with sharp cheddar cheese sauce and a crunchy buttered breadcrumb topping.
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C and butter a 23×33 cm baking dish.
Boil the macaroni in salted water for 1 minute less than package directions, then drain.
In a saucepan, melt 45 g butter, whisk in flour, and cook for 1 minute to make a blond roux.
Slowly whisk in the warm milk and simmer until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes.
Off the heat, stir in mustard, nutmeg, salt, and three-quarters of both cheeses until smooth.
Fold in the macaroni, transfer to the baking dish, and top with the remaining cheese mixed with panko and the rest of the butter, melted.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until bubbling and golden brown on top, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: New England Clam Chowder
recipes.description: Rich, creamy white chowder with tender clams, potatoes, and bacon, originating from coastal New England.

ingredients.name: fresh clams or canned chopped clams

steps: Render the bacon in a heavy pot over medium heat until crisp, then remove half for garnish.
Add onion and celery to the bacon fat and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle in the flour and stir for 1 minute to cook off the raw taste.
Whisk in the clam juice and milk gradually, then add the potatoes, bay leaf, and thyme.
Simmer until the potatoes are fork-tender, about 15 minutes.
Stir in the clams and heavy cream and warm through for 3–4 minutes without boiling, or the clams will toughen.
Discard the bay leaf, season with salt and pepper, and serve topped with the reserved bacon.

recipes.title: Cornbread
recipes.description: Golden, slightly sweet Southern-style cornbread baked in a cast iron skillet with a crisp crust.
steps: Place a 25 cm cast iron skillet in the oven and preheat to 220°C.
Whisk cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
In another bowl, whisk buttermilk, eggs, and 60 g of the melted butter.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and stir just until combined; do not overmix.
Carefully remove the hot skillet, swirl the remaining butter around to coat, and pour in the batter.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool 10 minutes before slicing into wedges.

recipes.title: Pecan Pie
recipes.description: Iconic Southern dessert pie with a buttery crust filled with toasted pecans in a sweet, gooey caramel-like filling.
steps: Preheat the oven to 175°C and place a baking sheet on the middle rack to catch drips.
Roll out the pie crust and fit into a 23 cm pie pan, crimping the edges.
Spread the pecans evenly over the crust.
Whisk corn syrup, brown sugar, melted butter, eggs, vanilla, salt, and bourbon if using until smooth.
Pour the filling over the pecans; the pecans will float to the top.
Bake on the preheated baking sheet for 50–60 minutes until the center is just set and only slightly jiggly.
Cool completely on a wire rack, at least 2 hours, before slicing.

recipes.title: Apple Pie
recipes.description: All-American double-crust pie filled with cinnamon-spiced apples in a buttery, flaky pastry.

ingredients.name: Granny Smith and Honeycrisp apples, peeled and sliced

steps: Toss apples with sugars, flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, and lemon juice; let sit 15 minutes.
Roll out one crust and fit into a 23 cm pie pan.
Spoon apples and accumulated juices into the crust and dot with butter cubes.
Cover with the second rolled crust, trim, crimp the edges, and cut steam vents.
Brush the top with egg wash and sprinkle with a little extra sugar.
Bake at 220°C for 20 minutes, then reduce to 190°C and bake another 40 minutes until the filling bubbles thickly through the vents.
Cool at least 3 hours so the filling sets before slicing.

recipes.title: Jambalaya
recipes.description: Louisiana Creole one-pot rice dish with chicken, andouille sausage, shrimp, and the holy trinity of vegetables.
steps: Brown the andouille sausage in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat, then remove with a slotted spoon.
Season chicken with half the Cajun seasoning and brown in the rendered fat; remove.
Add onion, bell pepper, and celery to the pot and cook until softened, about 6 minutes.
Stir in garlic, remaining Cajun seasoning, tomatoes, stock, bay leaves, sausage, and chicken.
Add the rice, stir once, bring to a boil, then cover and reduce to low for 20 minutes without stirring.
Stir in the shrimp, cover again, and cook another 5 minutes until the shrimp are pink and the rice is tender.
Discard the bay leaves and let rest 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Gumbo
recipes.description: Slow-cooked Louisiana stew built on a dark roux with chicken, andouille sausage, and the holy trinity, served over rice.
steps: Whisk flour and oil in a heavy pot over medium-low heat and stir constantly for 30–45 minutes until the roux is the color of dark chocolate.
Add onion, bell pepper, and celery to the roux and cook 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in garlic and Cajun seasoning and cook 1 minute.
Slowly whisk in the warm chicken stock and bring to a simmer.
Add the chicken thighs, sausage, and bay leaves, and simmer uncovered for 1 hour until the chicken is tender.
Shred the chicken, return to the pot, add okra, and simmer another 20 minutes.
Adjust seasoning and serve in bowls over hot white rice.

recipes.title: Po' Boy
recipes.description: Louisiana sandwich on French bread filled with crispy fried shrimp, lettuce, tomato, pickles, and remoulade sauce.
steps: Marinate shrimp in buttermilk with 1 tbsp Cajun seasoning for 15 minutes.
Whisk mayo, Creole mustard, hot sauce, and a pinch of Cajun seasoning to make the remoulade.
Combine cornmeal, flour, and remaining Cajun seasoning in a bowl.
Heat oil to 175°C, then dredge shrimp in the cornmeal mixture and fry in batches for 2–3 minutes until golden and crisp.
Drain on a wire rack.
Split the baguettes lengthwise, lightly toast cut sides, and spread generously with remoulade.
Layer in lettuce, tomato, pickles, and the hot fried shrimp, then cut and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Philly Cheesesteak
recipes.description: Iconic Philadelphia sandwich of thinly sliced ribeye with melted cheese and onions on a soft hoagie roll.

ingredients.name: ribeye steak, frozen briefly and shaved thin

steps: Heat a large flat skillet or griddle over high heat with the oil.
Add the onions and cook, stirring often, until soft and golden, about 8 minutes; push to one side.
Add the shaved ribeye to the hot side and chop it with two spatulas while it cooks for 2–3 minutes until just no longer pink.
Mix the meat with the onions and season with salt and pepper.
Divide the meat into 4 portions on the griddle and lay 2 slices of provolone over each, letting them melt.
Open the hoagie rolls cut-side down on top of each portion to warm through, about 30 seconds.
Use the spatulas to scoop each portion into its roll, then close and serve immediately.

recipes.title: BBQ Ribs
recipes.description: Tender, smoky pork baby back ribs slow-cooked with a dry rub and finished with sticky barbecue sauce.
steps: Preheat the oven to 135°C and remove the silver skin from the back of each rack.
Mix all dry spices and brown sugar to make the rub, then coat both sides of the ribs generously.
Wrap each rack tightly in foil with a splash of cider vinegar and place on a baking sheet.
Bake for 2.5 hours until the meat pulls back from the bones.
Open the foil, brush ribs with BBQ sauce on both sides, and broil or grill over medium heat for 6–8 minutes per side, basting with more sauce.
Rest the ribs 10 minutes before slicing between the bones.
Serve with extra warm BBQ sauce on the side.

recipes.title: Pulled Pork
recipes.description: Slow-cooked pork shoulder shredded and tossed in tangy barbecue sauce, perfect on buns with coleslaw.
steps: Combine all dry spices and brown sugar to make the rub.
Pat the pork dry and rub the spice mixture over every surface; refrigerate uncovered for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oven to 150°C and place the pork in a Dutch oven or roasting pan with the cider vinegar and stock.
Cover tightly and roast for 6–7 hours until a fork twists easily in the meat, about 95°C internal temperature.
Rest the pork 30 minutes, then shred with two forks, discarding large fat pieces.
Skim the fat off the pan juices and stir the juices back into the meat along with BBQ sauce.
Serve piled on soft buns with coleslaw and pickles.

recipes.title: Reuben Sandwich
recipes.description: Grilled rye sandwich layered with corned beef, Swiss cheese, sauerkraut, and Russian dressing.
steps: Whisk mayo, ketchup, relish, and mustard to make Russian dressing.
Squeeze the sauerkraut firmly to remove excess moisture, then warm gently in a pan to take off the chill.
Butter one side of every slice of rye bread.
On the unbuttered side of 4 slices, spread dressing, then layer Swiss cheese, warmed corned beef, sauerkraut, more cheese, and another spread of dressing.
Close with the remaining slices, butter side out.
Cook on a medium skillet for 3–4 minutes per side, pressing lightly, until the bread is deep golden and the cheese is fully melted.
Slice diagonally and serve with pickles.

recipes.title: Chicago-Style Deep Dish Pizza
recipes.description: Thick-crusted Chicago pizza with a buttery crust, layers of cheese, sausage, and chunky tomato sauce on top.
steps: Mix flour, cornmeal, yeast, salt, water, olive oil, and melted butter to form a dough; knead 6 minutes until smooth.
Cover and let rise 1 hour until doubled.
Simmer crushed tomatoes with garlic, oregano, and a pinch of salt for 15 minutes; cool.
Press the dough into a generously oiled 25 cm deep dish pan, pushing it up the sides about 5 cm.
Lay mozzarella slices to fully cover the bottom, scatter raw sausage in chunks over the cheese, and spoon the tomato sauce evenly on top.
Sprinkle Parmesan over the sauce and bake at 220°C for 30–35 minutes until the crust is deep golden.
Rest 10 minutes before slicing into thick wedges.

recipes.title: Cobb Salad
recipes.description: Composed American salad with crisp greens, chicken, bacon, blue cheese, avocado, tomato, and hard-boiled egg.
steps: Whisk vinegar, mustard, garlic, salt, and pepper, then slowly stream in olive oil to emulsify the dressing.
Spread the chopped romaine on a wide platter.
Arrange chicken, bacon, eggs, avocado, tomatoes, and blue cheese in distinct rows over the lettuce.
Drizzle the dressing over the top just before serving.
Toss tableside or have diners take their preferred mix of toppings.

recipes.title: Meatloaf
recipes.description: Classic American comfort dish of seasoned ground beef baked in a loaf shape with a tangy ketchup glaze.
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Soak breadcrumbs in milk for 5 minutes.
Sauté onion and garlic in a little oil until soft, then cool slightly.
Combine beef, soaked breadcrumbs, onion mixture, eggs, Worcestershire, mustard, salt, and pepper, mixing gently with hands until just combined.
Shape into a loaf about 23 cm long on the baking sheet.
Whisk ketchup, brown sugar, and vinegar, then brush half over the loaf.
Bake for 45 minutes, brush with remaining glaze, and bake another 15 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 70°C; rest 10 minutes before slicing.

recipes.title: Chicken Pot Pie
recipes.description: Flaky-crusted savory pie filled with creamy chicken, peas, carrots, and potatoes in a rich gravy.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Melt butter in a large skillet, brown the chicken until just cooked through, then remove.
Add onion, carrots, celery, and potato to the pan and cook 8 minutes until softened.
Sprinkle in the flour and stir 1 minute.
Whisk in stock and milk gradually, then simmer until the sauce thickens and the potato is tender, about 8 minutes.
Stir in peas, thyme, and chicken; season well and pour into a 23 cm pie dish.
Top with the puff pastry, trim the edges, cut steam vents, brush with egg wash, and bake 25–30 minutes until the pastry is deep golden and the filling bubbles.

recipes.title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipes.description: Classic American chewy cookies with crisp edges and pockets of melted chocolate chips.
steps: Whisk flour, baking soda, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl, whisk melted butter with both sugars until smooth.
Whisk in the whole egg, yolk, and vanilla until thick and ribbony.
Fold in the dry ingredients with a spatula just until no flour remains.
Stir in the chocolate chips, then chill the dough for at least 30 minutes.
Scoop 50 g balls onto parchment-lined sheets, leaving space to spread.
Bake at 175°C for 12–14 minutes until edges are golden but centers still look underdone; cool on the pan 5 minutes.

recipes.title: Caesar Salad
recipes.description: Crisp romaine tossed in a creamy garlic-anchovy dressing with Parmesan cheese and crunchy croutons.
steps: Toss bread cubes with 2 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and toast at 190°C for 10 minutes until golden, shaking the pan halfway.
Mash garlic and anchovies into a paste with the side of a knife.
Whisk the paste with yolks, lemon juice, mustard, and Worcestershire.
Slowly whisk in remaining olive oil to emulsify into a creamy dressing.
Stir in half the Parmesan and season with pepper.
Toss torn romaine with the dressing until evenly coated.
Top with croutons and the remaining Parmesan and serve right away.

recipes.title: New York Cheesecake
recipes.description: Dense, ultra-creamy New York-style cheesecake with a buttery graham cracker crust.
steps: Preheat the oven to 160°C and tightly wrap the outside of a 23 cm springform pan in foil.
Mix graham crumbs, melted butter, and 30 g sugar; press firmly into the bottom of the pan and bake 10 minutes; cool.
Beat cream cheese on medium until smooth, scraping down the bowl.
Add sugar and flour and beat to combine, then mix in sour cream, vanilla, and lemon zest.
Beat in eggs one at a time on low speed, just until incorporated.
Pour the batter onto the crust and place the pan in a roasting tray; pour hot water halfway up the sides.
Bake 60–70 minutes until edges are set but the center wobbles slightly; turn off oven and let cool inside 1 hour, then refrigerate at least 6 hours before slicing.

recipes.title: Sloppy Joe
recipes.description: Saucy ground beef simmered with onions, peppers, and a tangy tomato sauce, served piled on soft hamburger buns.
steps: Brown the ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart, until no pink remains; drain excess fat.
Add onion, bell pepper, and garlic and cook 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in tomato sauce, ketchup, brown sugar, Worcestershire, mustard, chili powder, and smoked paprika.
Simmer uncovered for 12–15 minutes until thick enough to mound on a spoon.
Season with salt and pepper.
Toast the buns and spoon the meat mixture generously over the bottom halves.
Cap with the top bun and serve immediately, ideally with pickles.

recipes.title: Buffalo Chicken Dip
recipes.description: Creamy, cheesy, spicy baked dip loaded with shredded chicken and Buffalo hot sauce, served warm with chips.
steps: Preheat the oven to 175°C.
Beat the cream cheese in a bowl until smooth and easy to spread.
Stir in hot sauce and ranch dressing until evenly combined.
Fold in the shredded chicken and half the cheddar.
Spread into a 23 cm baking dish and top with the remaining cheddar and the blue cheese.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until bubbling and lightly browned on top.
Sprinkle with green onions and serve hot with tortilla chips and celery sticks.

recipes.title: Banana Bread
recipes.description: Moist American quick bread made with very ripe bananas, brown sugar, and warm spices.
steps: Preheat the oven to 175°C and grease a 23×13 cm loaf pan.
Whisk mashed bananas with melted butter, brown sugar, eggs, and vanilla.
In another bowl whisk flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
Fold the dry ingredients into the wet just until no streaks of flour remain.
Stir in walnuts if using.
Pour into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
Bake 55–65 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean; cool 15 minutes before slicing.

recipes.title: Buttermilk Pancakes
recipes.description: Fluffy, golden American breakfast pancakes made with tangy buttermilk, served with butter and maple syrup.
steps: Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl, whisk buttermilk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla.
Pour wet into dry and stir just until combined; lumps are fine and overmixing makes pancakes tough.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly grease.
Pour 80 ml batter per pancake and cook until bubbles form and pop on the surface, about 2 minutes.
Flip and cook another 1–2 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Serve hot with butter and warmed maple syrup.
